intended_use: ME-APDS (Magentiq Eye's Automatic Polyp Detection System) is intended to be used by endoscopists as an adjunct to the common video colonoscopy procedure (screening and surveillance), aiming to assist
intended_use_full: ME-APDS (Magentiq Eye's Automatic Polyp Detection System) is intended to be used by endoscopists as an adjunct to the common video colonoscopy procedure (screening and surveillance), aiming to assist the endoscopist in identifying lesions during colonoscopy procedure by highlighting regions with visual characteristics consistent with different types of mucosal abnormalities that appear in the colonoscopy video during the procedure. Highlighted regions can be independently assessed by the endoscopist and appropriate action taken according to standard clinical practice.
ground_truth: histopathological findings; 375 polyps had histology results (302 adenoma polyps identified)
other_metrics: Frame-wise Recall (FRecall): 73.0% [70.58%, 75.65%]; Polyp-wise Recall at 1 frame (PRecall1): 98.1% [97.0%, 99.06%]; Polyp-wise Recall at 3 frames (PRecall3): 95.8% [94.12%, 97.24%]; Polyp-wise Recall at 5 frames (PRecall5): 93.8% [91.81%, 95.46%]; Polyp-wise Recall at 7 frames (PRecall7): 91.2% [89.02%, 93.14%]; Median Polyp Coverage (MPC): 72.0%; False Positives Per Frame (FPPF): 0.0334; Tool Detection Frame-wise Recall: 85.9% [83.21%, 88.83%]; Tool Detection Polyp-wise Recall at 1 frame: 99.7% [99.15%, 100%]; Tool Detection False Positives Per Frame: 0.0118
